categories.name: Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP)

questions.text: Can staff give an example of what a critical control point (CCP) is? (ask staff members)
Are staff fully aware of the corrective actions to be taken as stated in the HACCP if a fault occurs? (ask staff members)
Any current issues with goods being delivered
Are products rejected if damaged, out of date, or delivered at incorrect temperature?
Are chemicals delivered and stored separately from food products?
Is the meat divider in use?
Is prep area stock rotated correctly and not out of date (include sauces/meats/drinks)?
Are all fridge door seals clean (inside fold) and intact?

Calibrate the probe:
Is the probe within 0.5°C of the test cap? If not contact head office immediately.
Is food always covered and labelled when storing overnight? Use by date.
Is food correctly loaded in the fridge i.e. raw at the bottom, cooked at the top?
Is the shop probe calibrated every 3 months using test caps and records up to date?

Measure the temperatures of all of the below. Are all fridges below 8°C and all freezers below -18°C?
If no, contact maintenance and head office must be informed immediately.
Measure the refrigerated temperature of:savoury product and cream product Are both below 8°C? If not contact head office immediately.
Are daily temperature sheets completed?
Are all maintenance issues regarding refrigeration logged in shire
Cream Chiller: Left Back
Cream Chiller Left Front
Cream Chiller Front Centre
Cream Chiller Right Front
Cream Chiller Right Back
Cream Chiller Centre Back
Savoury Chiller Left Back
Savoury Left Front
Savoury Centre Front
Savoury Chiller Front Right
Savoury Chiller Back Right
Savoury Chiller Back Centre
Are all staff aware of the 4-hour rule for sandwiches? Is it in practice if required (ask staff members)
Are all items stored off the floor?
Are all staff aware of the two hour rule for bake off? (ask staff members)
Ask a staff member, what is the minimum product temperature for holding hot sausage and hot roast pork? Did they answer above 63°C?
Ask a staff member, what minimum temperature should products be baked to? Did they answer above 75°C?
Check the temperature of a product leaving the oven. Is it in line with the temperatures above?
Is the bake-off temperature being recorded by the shop and is this up to date?
What product has been taken to sample?
When was product cooked and how long for?
Probe product. What is the internal temperature reading at?
Take photo of product and cut open. Does the inside of product meet company standards?
Are utensils used for cooked / raw meats stored separately and identifiable?
Are hands always washed immediately before and after handling raw product (including raw bake off)?
Is the probe wiped with an antibacterial wipe after each use?
Is all the correct signage in use: NOW WASH HANDS after toilets/restrooms  HAND WASH/ UTENSIL WASH ONLY above sinks  NO SMOKING at shop door NO DOGS at shop door Hairnets/Hair coverings
Handwash sinks do not have utensils in them.

Handwash sinks are clean, have hot water, soap and a towel dispenser.
In the event any of the above are missing, contact maintenance and head office.
Staff are dressed correctly and clothing is clean.
Does the manager know the procedure on staff illness, sickness and diarrhea? (ask manager)
Glass on display units is intact, particularly on edges. If no, report to maintenance immediately.
Is the manager aware of the glass breakage procedure? Do they have a glass clean up kit?
Are there NO foreign body threats? (shelves above prep, holes in walls, loose screws, damaged surfaces, etc.)
Are there NO outstanding pest control issues?
Are there any areas of the shop that are not being cleaned correctly?
Are the structure and fittings smooth and easy to clean?
Is there an adequate stock of chemicals? (not overstock)
Are chemicals being stored away from food / packaging?
Floors under equipment such as fridges is free from debris?
All waste is in lidded bins and areas are tidy?
Do staff weara hairnet and wash hands when preparing food in prep area?
Are boards being switched and surfaces being cleaned between changing fillings for filled rolls and sandwiches?
Is the temperature file/check book readily available and all checks up to date
Calibrate the shop scales: Are all scales in good working order? Report any faults to maintenance immediately.
Are staff aware of the product recall procedure? (ask staff members)
Is the shop generally clean and tidy?
Are floors free from obstructions and trip hazards e.g. clutter, trailing cables?
BOH, are floors in good condition and non-slip
Is there adequate storage for materials and equipment?
Are boxes/items stored to prevent objects falling/collapsing?
Is racking/storage shelving secure, stable and in good condition?
Is proof of employers liability insurance on display and is it in date?
Is there a health and safety law poster on display with the relevant details included?
Are there no sharp edges which could cause injury?
Can the manager on shift tell you where the water/electricity cut-off is? Are they labelled?
If there are glazed doors/fronts is there sufficient logos or bands?
Do doors open without introducing risks to customers/public?
Is there a sign offering customers assistance if required?
Is there adequate ventilation to remove fumes e.g. oven steam?
Is the staff toilet functioning and clean with adequate handwashing facilities?
Are there suitable break facilities including seating, facilities for having a meal and hot water?
Are there any obvious signs of wear and tear or damage that could affect the safety of the machinery or equipment?
Are hazardous substances stored safely and securely?
Are hazardous substances in appropriate containers and not decanted in unmarked containers?
Are there COSHH assessments and safety data sheets available for all hazardous substances? (no personal/unapproved chemicals on site)
If manual handling is observed during the inspection visit, do staff lift with the correct technique?
Is there adequate PPE on site including oven gloves, rhino sleeves, disposable gloves and goggles?
If observed, do staff use the PPE required for the task (as stipulated in the applicable assessment)?
Are contact details for the mental health first aiders/advocates and other resources displayed in an area visible to all employees?
Have all incidents of violence and aggression been reported back to head office?
Are the risks of violence and aggression adequately controlled? (discuss with manager)
Has an asbestos survey been carried out where appropriate and is it available to internal maintenance engineers and external contractors?
Are any asbestos containing materials clearly labelled?
Is there a suitable and sufficient legionella risk assessment dated within the last 2 years?
Are control measures as specified in the legionella risk assessment adhered to?
Is there a specific risk assessment in place for any vulnerable persons including new and expectant mothers and those with physical or mental health illnesses or disabilities?
Are any portable heating appliances fixed at a safe distance away from combustible materials and suitably guarded? Have current PAT test?
Are external waste disposal bins stored a minimum of 6m away from any part of the premises?
Are there NO multiplug adaptors/extension leads in use?
Does the shop have necessary call point test keys
Are there NO light bulbs and fittings next to flammable materials?
Is there NO visibly damaged/faulty electrical equipment?Has it been reported to maintenance?
Is there NO security issues which increase the risk of arson?
Are any flammable substances stored safely?
Does all furniture upholstery comply with current fire regulations?
Is there NO build up of combustibles inside the property? (e.g. cardboard, paper)
Is there NO build up of combustibles outside the property? (e.g. cardboard, wood)
Walls and ceiling are NOT covered with flammable linings? (e.g. polystyrene panels)
Is oven extractor clean and fully operational
Is there NO unnecessary build up of other combustibles (e.g. furniture, fabrics, artificial foliage, decorations)?
Are there NO unsealed holes in ceilings, walls, doors which would increase the rate of fire spread (fire stopping)? E.g. around pipework, cabling
Can all fire exits be opened immediately and easily? If electronic locks are fitted is there a functioning release button?
Do fire exit doors open in the direction of escape?
Is the risk adequately controlled if the fire exit leads to a change in level? E.g. ramp, warning label
Are escape routes clear of obstruction?
Are the correct directional fire exit and fire exit signs displayed?
Are luminaires and exit signs in good condition and undamaged?
Are external escape stairs in good condition and non-slip? (if applicable)
Are exit routes to the required width? (≥750 mm, or ≥900 mm if wheelchair users may be present)
Is the exit travel distance suitable?
Are fire doors closed and not propped open?
Do all fire doors display a “fire door keep closed” sign?
Are all fire doors fitted with intumescent strips and smoke seals?
Are there NO large gaps between the fire doors and frames (report anything thicker than a £1 coin)?

categories.name: Fire Alarms, Detection and Emergency Lighting

questions.text: Is a suitable fire alarm system installed which provides detection across the premises and alerts all employees and tenants (if applicable) in the event of a fire?
Is there sufficient emergency lighting? (illuminating rooms and escape routes)
Is the fire alarm panel easily accessible and visually undamaged?
Is there a zone map next to the fire alarm panel?
Are detectors NOT covered and visibly undamaged?
Are there break glass call points at suitable points? (exits)
Are there suitable and sufficient fire extinguishers present?
Are extinguishers secured to the wall or in a suitable stand?
Are there instruction signs adjacent to all fire extinguishers?
If a sprinkler system is present is the panel clearly labelled?
Is there a copy of the most recent fire safety policy available to staff (online training platform)?
Is there a fire emergency plan in place in line with Birds Fire Safety Policy?
Are staff, including managers, suitably trained in fire safety? (online training modules)
Are fire evacuation drills carried out at least every 3 months and recorded?
Are there special arrangements (Personal Emergency Evacuation Plan (PEEP) in place for high-risk personnel e.g. disabled employees, lone workers?
Is there a fire action notice clearly displayed in the shop which is visible to customers?
Is an up to date, suitable and sufficient fire risk assessment which is available to staff? (review at least every 2 years or upon significant change)
Has portable electrical equipment been tested in the past 24 months (PAT)?
Has fixed electrical testing been undertaken in the past 5 years?
Has the fire alarm system been serviced by a competent person in the past 6 months?
Has the emergency lighting been drain tested by a competent person in the past 6 months?
Have the fire extinguishers/blankets been serviced by a competent person in the past 12 months?
If present, has the sprinkler system been serviced by a competent person in the past 6 months?
Have refrigeration units been serviced within the past 12 months?
Have air conditioning units been serviced within the past 12 months?
Has the fire alarm been tested by staff weekly and recorded, rotating the call point each week?
Have extinguishers/blankets been visually checked monthly and recorded?
Has the emergency lighting been flick tested monthly and recorded?
Has the sprinkler system been tested by staff and recorded every 3 months?
Within viewing range how many other stores offer coffee?
Any signs/POS that the store sells coffee?
Coffee machine visible from outside?
Is there a tea urn in the store?
Where is the coffee machine located?
Any POS advertising coffee?
Enough cups, lids, sugar etc. accessible?
Observe service – are customers offered hot drinks? How?
How many coffees have been made before time of visit?
Hopper appropriately filled with beans?
Do the beans look/smell fresh?
Coffee station clean and tidy?
Is the hopper clean?
Open beans kept in an airtight container?
Container labelled and dated?
Brew time of double espresso (21–25s)?
Double espresso weight (45–50g)?
If out of range, did fresh beans correct the issue?
Machine recently serviced?
Any current issues with the coffee machine?
Is the ice machine clean?
Is the external part of the building well presented (e.g. no cracks, slip or trip hazards)?
Is there a sign offering customer assistance?
Is there a sign clearly stating opening times?
Is there a member of staff present behind the bar and/or on the shop floor?
Is the internal flooring in good condition?
Is all internal and external signage in good condition and clearly readable?
Are all products well displayed?
Are any products broken or damaged and requiring removal?
Do all products have tickets clearly displayed and in good condition?
Are all customer products in date and displayed in the correct date order?
Are all lights working and adequately illuminating the customer areas?
Is the air conditioning clean and fully operational?
Are all customer-facing surfaces and floors free from dust and debris?
Are customer toilets clean, well stocked and is the door fully operational?
If tables and chairs are provided, are they neatly arranged and in good working condition?
What are the hot plate settings
What is the heat lamp dial set to
FOH: what are the fridges set to
BOH: what are the fridges set to
Aircon: what are they set to
Do all members of management have current Level 3 Food Safety
Do all team members have current Level 2 Food Safety
Please click close button
